question: Describe the structure of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component that forms part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, supporting various internal systems and ensuring the containment of fission products. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies that the reactor vessel must be shipped and stored under controlled, dry, and pressurized conditions to maintain integrity, and lists the key codes and standards used for compliance.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits under anticipated operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component designed to prevent the release of fission products, and its materials are selected and fabricated according to stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity throughout the plant’s lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed in accordance with regulatory definitions and requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the use of low alloy steel, various stainless steels, and nickel-based alloys, all in accordance with relevant BPVC Section II material specifications, to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, plenum access ports, and thermal relief valves, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs and contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various system elements, and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

edges.description: Section A provides a general description of the reactor vessel's structure, while Section B references specific inspection, shipment, and installation requirements that apply to the reactor vessel described in Section A.

Section A describes the physical structure and components of the reactor vessel, which is the subject for which Section B outlines the operational procedures and integrity requirements to ensure its safe operation within specified limits.
Section B specifies that the pressurizer is an integral part of the reactor vessel described in Section A, detailing its location and structural integration within the vessel.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor vessel's structure, while Section B specifies the materials used in constructing that structure, connecting the physical design to its material composition.

Section A describes the shipment, handling, and storage requirements for the reactor vessel, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure vessel integrity during operation; both sections are related through their focus on maintaining the structural integrity and compliance of the reactor vessel throughout its lifecycle.
Section B specifies that the pressurizer is an integral part of the reactor vessel described in Section A, detailing a structural component within the vessel.

Section B provides the general structural description and components of the reactor vessel, while Section A references inspection, shipment, and handling requirements that apply to the vessel described in Section B.
Section B specifies that the pressurizer is an integral structural component of the reactor vessel described in Section A, detailing its location and physical relationship within the vessel.

Section B provides a general physical and structural description of the reactor vessel, while Section A discusses operational procedures developed to maintain the vessel's integrity within specified limits, thus Section A's procedures are based on the vessel structure described in Section B.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor vessel and its components, including the pressurizer, thus Section A specifies details for the general structure described in Section B.
Section B provides specific structural details about the reactor vessel, which is identified in Section A as a key component maintaining the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.
Section A defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the reactor vessel whose shipment and installation requirements are detailed in Section B.

Section A defines the integrity requirements and scope for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the reactor vessel, while Section B discusses the operational procedures developed to maintain the integrity of the reactor vessel within those defined limits.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of specific components and materials (such as the RPV upper shell, upper head, and baffle plate) that are part of the overall reactor vessel structure described more generally in Section B.

Section A specifies the materials and structural components of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) upper shell and head, which are part of the overall reactor vessel whose shipment, installation, and inspection requirements are described in Section B.

Section A describes specific structural components and materials of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are foundational to understanding the vessel's integrity as discussed in Section B's focus on operational procedures and compliance.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor pressure vessel's contents, including the steam generators, while Section B gives a more detailed structural description of the reactor vessel and its integration within the NuScale Power Module.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel's contents and associated systems, while Section B specifies requirements for the shipment, installation, and inspection of the reactor vessel, thus detailing aspects of its handling and regulatory compliance.

Section A describes the physical components housed within the reactor vessel, while Section B addresses the operational procedures developed to maintain the vessel's integrity under specified conditions, both focusing on different aspects of the reactor vessel's structure and safe operation.

Section A provides detailed information about specific components and materials of the reactor vessel's pressurizer, which is one of the structural elements broadly listed as part of the reactor vessel in Section B's general description.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, identifying the reactor pressure vessel as a key component, while Section B gives specific structural details about the reactor vessel itself within the system.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor vessel as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B references specific requirements and procedures for the shipment, handling, and inspection of the reactor vessel, thus providing detailed specifications for the general description in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor vessel as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B discusses operational procedures to maintain the integrity of the reactor vessel under specified conditions, connecting them through the focus on the reactor vessel's structure and its safe operation.

Section A provides a general description of system components and references the reactor vessel, while Section B gives the specific structural details of the reactor vessel itself, connecting them through the shared focus on the reactor vessel's role and configuration.

Section A describes operational procedures involving the reactor vessel and its associated systems, while Section B provides references and requirements for the reactor vessel's inspection, shipment, and storage, connecting through their focus on the reactor vessel's structure and handling.

Section A describes system components and operational steps involving the reactor vessel, while Section B outlines procedural requirements to ensure the reactor vessel's pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, connecting them through the operational context and safety constraints applied to the vessel's structure.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor vessel as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B details specific operational procedures and components (such as the pressurizer and degasification line) that are part of the reactor vessel system.
